title: Sales Team Scorecard — October 2025 (current)
purpose: The active sales-team KPI scorecard. Defines what Comeketo sales performance is measured on, how each metric is weighted, and where each of the four salespeople currently stands. This is the source of truth for 'how is Andre doing' and 'who's in quota.'

source.file: rawdata/LATEST Lead Distribution - Scoreboard - Scorecoard Sales KPI - Comeketo(COpied October 2025 - Current_Scores_Use_This_One_Only.csv
source.import_note: CSV had multi-line wrapped headers and binary-hit vs raw-value columns mixed together. Decoded into explicit typed fields. Columns tagged 'not used' in source are omitted here.

scoring_framework.narrative: Score = sum of points from each KPI × performance multiplier. ~50% of the score is Win Rate. Activity (calls, hours) carries heavy weight. Rotting and dormant leads act as penalties. Sales velocity and avg-value-per-win pay bonuses against team average.

win_rate_90d_pct.note: Single largest driver of score. ~50% of total per Rule 4. Binary hit-or-miss against team average; raw percentage not stored in this sheet.

lead_connect_rate_30d_pct.note: Binary hit against team average; no points currently awarded in scored period.

connect_to_tasting_rate_30d_pct.note: Binary hit against team average; no points currently awarded in scored period.

tastings_attended_this_month.note: Setter-side. Quota is 1 per month for a full-timer (low bar — real differentiation is in last-month tastings-showed).

deals_closed_this_month.note: Raw count → points. Toni's 5 this-month deals paid 800 pts.

tastings_showed_last_month.note: Setter-side from last month. Recognition metric — doesn't move forward pipeline but acknowledges recent work.

closed_business_90d_usd.note: Last Month Performance recognition. Dollars closed rolling-90-day.

hours_worked.note: Below quota → heavy penalty (-100 or worse). Above quota → modest bonus.

activity_level.note: Composite activity score from CRM (likely Close). Quota 59.

dials_made.note: Rule 6 — 3× score. Prospecting metric. Eduarda's 1 dial is a load-bearing signal.

ceu_bonus.note: Continuing-education bonus. Not checked weekly — independent of the core KPI cadence.

rotting_leads_quantity.note: Count of rotting leads. Penalty scales with quantity.

sales_velocity_90d_usd.note: Rule 9 — 8× score. 'Counts as you collect cash.' Compared against team 90d average of ~$3,013.

total_reviews.note: Reviews from closed events. 0 reviews = -67 penalty. 1 review = +133 bonus. Only Ren has a positive in the current period.

weeks_hit_consistent_performance.note: Inbox-zero / weekly-hit streak. Resets quarterly. Currently 0 across the team.

rules: Lead assignment is only done via Camila or Domenic.
Deleting leads is strictly not allowed.
Deleting opportunities is strictly not allowed.
Win Rate has a 10× Score — it makes up approximately 50% of total score.
Tasting Show Rate & Booked Tastings have a 2× Score.
Calls are on the scorecard because salespeople should also be good at prospecting — 3× score.
Focus will always be on the core KPI of win rate.
If you are part-time and reach #1, you need to be online (green light) to get the perks and lead sharing.
Sales velocity is an 8× score. It counts as you collect cash.

bonus_price_rise.trigger: Tied to Bonus column — appears to be a team-wide price-adjustment tied to performance.

read: Leading the team by every performance lens: highest win-rate hit, highest this-month deals, and most consistent last-month output.
Only salesperson above the 40-hour quota — 47.4 hours earned the +100 effort bonus while the other three took -100 penalties.
Sales velocity $6,700 beats team avg $3,013 — bonus maxed at +200.
Zero reviews is the one soft spot — currently -67 penalty. Post-event follow-up is the cheap score left on the table.
Activity king — 145 dials is the highest on the team and drove +495 effort points.
Highest this-month deal count (5) and tied for highest last-month tastings (10) — strongest pipeline motion.
Velocity and value-per-win both below team average, suggesting smaller deal sizes despite volume.
Highest 90-day closed business on the team ($68,984) — big-deal closer.
But only 17.6 hours worked, 33 activity, and ONE dial in the period — the profile is whale-hunting, not motion.
Velocity and avg-value bonuses both maxed (+200 / +144). She wins big when she does win.
If she's part-time, rule 8 applies; if full-time, the effort gap is a genuine coaching conversation.
Only salesperson on the team with a positive review score this period (+133) — post-event follow-through is his edge.
Smallest 90-day closed biz ($5,382) and weakest velocity ($240) — closing on the low end and slowly.
Activity 55 (near 59 quota) and dials 65 (below 88 quota) — effort is there but not quite quota; perf multiplier 0.80× reflects the gap.

full_time_quotas.note: Source row lists all four as full-time. Rule 8 covers part-time case.

downstream_hooks: people/andre_raw.json carries Andre's identity + posture. This knowledge file carries his current numbers. Keep scorecard snapshots as new knowledge files (sales_team_scorecard_<period>.json) rather than mutating this one — the history matters.
When scoring Andre on the north_star relationship-maintenance regime, this scorecard is the evidence layer.
Rotting/dormant lead counts here are the ONLY lead-hygiene signal in the index today. If a Close CRM feed comes online later, reconcile the raw counts against this scorecard snapshot.
The comeketo-sales-oracle plugin (andre-coaching skill) references Andre's performance data — this file is the local mirror of that source of truth.
